author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session began with a QA pass before implementing changes to ensure the pipeline — especially the Discovery Gap Sentinel agent — would not be broken. Then ran a logic inspector audit followed by GitHub Actions workflow dispatch attempts. Two runs were triggered and one was cancelled. Reviewed audit findings, assessed risks, and then implemented fixes. Expanded the appPackage/declarative agent to handle more team needs (summarization, status quotes, drafting updates). Confirmed that Flow 1 and Flow 2 were working. Worked on defining Flow 3 (report upload to SharePoint document library via Power Automate HTTP trigger). Session ended while reviewing IDeaS custom GitHub Actions documentation explaining why workflow custom actions must be wrapped in reusable workflows — confirmed this was about org policy, not the runner failure.
problems: GitHub Actions runner was failing to acquire a hosted runner — workflow_dispatch jobs waiting indefinitely then failing with internal server error
Standard GitHub-hosted runners had no status badge in the enterprise EMU org, suggesting runner provisioning was not set up
Declarative agent could not read gap reports from SharePoint because reports were still GitHub Actions artifacts, not SharePoint document library files
IDeaS custom GitHub Actions policy requires custom actions to be wrapped in reusable workflows — direct use is prohibited
approaches: Ran logic inspector audit on pipeline critical files, then QA'd findings before proposing fixes
Triggered two GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch runs to test runner acquisition (one cancelled)
Reviewed IDeaS GitHub Actions custom action documentation to understand org-level workflow constraints
Drafted Flow 3 definition: Power Automate HTTP trigger receives artifact from GitHub, writes to SharePoint document library
Identified that the repo under personal EMU account was not in the org-level runner provisioning scope
results: Runner failure diagnosed: repo was under personal EMU account, not ideasorg — outside runner provisioning scope
IDeaS workflow structure requirement understood: custom actions must go inside reusable workflows
Flow 3 spec drafted for report upload
Declarative agent functionality expanded to cover summarization, status reporting, and drafting

decisions.decision: Repo must be under ideasorg (not personal EMU account) to get GitHub-hosted runners
Flow 3 should use a Power Automate HTTP trigger — no admin OAuth registration needed

open_questions: How do we migrate or fork the repo from personal EMU account to ideasorg to get runner access?
What is the exact SharePoint document library name to use for report uploads?
